text: <par>WT <latinform>mar</latinform> 'butter', loan from Indo-Aryan <emph>marśa-</emph> 'rubbing' (cf. Turner #9896, p. 569). This word spread from Tibetan into other TB languages’ words for oil, etc.</par>

<par>The credit for recognizing the cognacy of this Chinese word with forms in Tibetan belongs to G.H. Luce (1981). See Matisoff 1983 #52. The labial initial is paralleled in WT <latinform>sbrum-pa</latinform> 'pregnant'.</par>

<par>This word is evidently a loan from Burmese, since PTB <latinform>*-k</latinform> regularly became <latinform>-ʔ</latinform> in Jingpho. The other element in this elaborate expression, however, is from Shan (cf. Siamese <latinform>jâak</latinform>).</par>
